panels.title: Availability (API requests, non-5xx) — target 99.5% monthly
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: Availability (API + frontend), 99.5% monthly. Proxy here is the non-5xx share of apigateway HTTP requests (otelhttp's http_server_request_duration_seconds_count); frontend static-asset availability is not separately instrumented yet.

panels.title: Data freshness P95 — target ≤ 15s
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: Data freshness (P95) ≤ 15s behind source, measured per source and end-to-end. skyradar_normalizer_freshness_seconds is the source-observation-to-publish gap; skyradar_adapter_source_freshness_seconds is seconds since each adapter's last successful poll.

panels.title: Event detection latency P95 — target ≤ 5s
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: Event detection latency (P95) ≤ 5s from normalized ingest to event emission. Stale-signal events (synthesized by the periodic sweep, not triggered by a single flights.updates message) contribute to the emitted-events series but not the latency histogram.

options.content:
**Master PRD SLO:** Map interaction latency (pan/zoom/filter) ≤ 150ms on a mid-tier device.

This is a frontend perf budget profiled in CI via Lighthouse (see `docs/tech-stack/observability-and-ops.md`'s CI quality gates), not a backend metric — there is no live RUM panel here yet. Tracked as a CI gate, not on this dashboard, until frontend telemetry is added.

panels.title: API latency P95, live/cache-eligible reads (/flights*) — target ≤ 200ms
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: API latency (P95, cached reads) ≤ 200ms. GET /flights and /flights/{icao24} are the viewport/detail reads this target applies to; response caching itself is not implemented yet (see docs/tech-stack/backend.md), so this currently measures uncached Redis-hot-state reads.

panels.title: API latency P95, history queries (/replay) — target ≤ 800ms
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: API latency (P95, uncached/history queries) ≤ 800ms. GET /replay is this surface's only history/uncached query route today.

panels.title: WebSocket fan-out volume
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: WebSocket fan-out — per-connection bandwidth bounded by viewport regardless of global aircraft count. The bound itself is enforced by wsHub.broadcast's per-client viewport filter and verified by the manual small-bbox-vs-large-bbox test in docs/prd/phase-2-realtime-systems.md; this panel is a live volume sanity signal, not a per-connection bandwidth measurement.

options.content:
**Master PRD SLO:** Cache hit rate (viewport/query reads) ≥ 60%.

**Status: not yet measurable.** Redis-backed response caching for viewport/detail queries (`docs/tech-stack/backend.md`) has not been implemented yet — GET /flights and /flights/{icao24} currently read Redis hot state directly on every request. Once caching ships, instrument it as `skyradar_api_cache_requests_total{result="hit|miss"}` and replace this panel with `sum(rate(...{result="hit"}[5m])) / sum(rate(...[5m]))`.

panels.title: Disaster recovery — RPO (durable-write lag) — target ≤ 5min
panels.description: Master PRD SLO: Disaster recovery RPO ≤ 5 minutes for durable history/event store. skyradar_pgstorewriter_write_lag_seconds (gap between a flights.updates/events.detected message's publish/occurrence time and its durable Postgres write) is a continuous proxy for how much data a crash right now could lose — not a substitute for an actual DR drill (Phase 3).

options.content:
**Master PRD SLO:** Disaster recovery RTO ≤ 1 hour for full service restore.

**Status: not yet measured.** RTO can only be measured by actually executing a restore; this is explicitly a Phase 3 deliverable (`docs/prd/phase-3-reliability-and-scale.md`'s DR drill), out of scope for this observability milestone. Tracked here as a placeholder so the SLO stays visible until a real number exists.

panels.title: Security — no critical/high CVEs for > 7 days

options.content:
**Master PRD SLO:** No known critical/high CVEs in production dependencies for > 7 days, automated scanning on every build.

**Status: not yet measured.** This is a CI-time signal (govulncheck/Trivy), not a runtime metric exported by any service; the CI quality gate itself is listed as planned in `docs/tech-stack/observability-and-ops.md` and is not yet wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Once it is, export the scan result as a metric (e.g. via a CI-pushed Pushgateway sample) to replace this placeholder.

**Master PRD SLO:** Stay within declared free-tier/sponsor-funded budget; alert before the cap is hit.

**Status: not yet measured.** The declared cap is a flat **$6/month DigitalOcean droplet** (`docs/tech-stack/hosting-and-deployment.md`). No cloud billing/usage API integration exists yet to turn that into a live burn-rate metric or alert (`docs/tech-stack/observability-and-ops.md`'s "Cost alerting" section describes this as dependent on whatever billing API is available) — tracked here as a placeholder.
